title: Achillea kraettliana
intro: Achillea kraettliana, also known as white yarrow or Alpine yarrow, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Central Europe and the Balkans. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which have been attributed to its potential health benefits in treating digestive issues, fever, and menstrual cramps. The bioactive constituents of Achillea kraettliana include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Achillea kraettliana include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to support digestive health and relieve menstrual discomfort.
taxonomy: Achillea kraettliana is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Achillea, commonly known as yarrow. The genus Achillea belongs to the subfamily Anthemidoideae and is native to the Mediterranean region. Within the family Asteraceae, Achillea kraettliana is part of the order Asterales and the clade Eudicots. The classification of Achillea kraettliana reflects its evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.

constituents: Achillea kraettliana contains sesquiterpene lactones, primarily 11beta-hydroxydehydrokrae, which are known for their bioactive properties. These compounds are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. The sesquiterpene lactones in Achillea kraettliana have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities.
